channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 2 hours.

item.title: 🌏 The Quad should share intel on critical-mineral supply risks | ASPI Strategist
item.description: With China increasing restrictions on critical minerals trade, including tighter export controls on rare earth elements announced in March, the United States and allied countries are trying to bring more mineral supply to market. To ...

item.title: 🌍 Israel Now Using Netting To Protect Combat Vehicles Against Scourge Of Hezbollah Drones | The War Zone
item.description: The netting appears as the IDF takes heat for not doing more to defend against FPV drone attacks, although options remain very limited. The post Israel Now Using Netting To Protect Combat Vehicles Against Scourge Of Hezbollah Drones appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ US Navy to extend service life of amphibious assault ship USS Wasp by 5 years | Defense News
item.description: The service completed a study of Wasp-class amphibious assault ships and is currently conducting another on amphibious dock landing ships.

item.title: 🏛️ Confidence In CH-53K King Stallion Grows Ahead Of First Operational Deployment | The War Zone
item.description: Going to sea with the 26th Marine Expeditionary Unit is a prelude to the CH-53K hitting full-rate production, planned for FY 2029. The post Confidence In CH-53K King Stallion Grows Ahead Of First Operational Deployment appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Key takeaways from Hegseth’s day in front of the House | Breaking Defense
item.description: What to know about Pete Hegseth’s appearance in front of the House Armed Services Committee.

item.title: 🌍 Where Is Iran’s Enriched Uranium? | Foreign Policy
item.description: The U.N. nuclear watchdog suspects that roughly half of Tehran’s stockpile is still at its Isfahan facility.

item.title: 🏛️ ‘Resign Immediately’: Democrats Grill Hegseth on Iran War | Foreign Policy
item.description: Congressional Democrats fought with the U.S. defense secretary over the war and military spending in a rare public hearing.

item.title: 🌏 A blueprint for a US-South Korea combined multi-domain task force | Atlantic Council
item.description: A combined multi-domain task force would fill a critical gap in the US-ROK alliance’s deterrence and rapid-response capabilities. The post A blueprint for a US-South Korea combined multi-domain task force appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ Marine Corps considering Army’s MV-75 as an attack helo replacement | Defense One
item.description: Bell unveiled an armed version specifically for the Corps at the Modern Day Marine conference.

item.title: 📍 India Rethinks Energy Security Amid War | Foreign Policy
item.description: As New Delhi feels the crunch, diplomacy in Abu Dhabi hints at expanding green cooperation.

item.title: 🌍 Don’t call it a ‘quagmire’: defense secretary refuses to speculate on length of Iran war | Defense News
item.description: The questions over the war’s future come as a 60-day deadline approaches Thursday under the War Powers Act.

item.title: 📍 Where Is Pakistan Again? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Why the World Bank’s quiet decision to move the South Asian country to its Middle East column is more than just a bureaucratic footnote.

item.title: 🏴 Trump says he discussed a Ukraine ceasefire with Putin | Defense News
item.description: Trump said he suggested "a little bit of a ceasefire" in the war in Ukraine during his phone call Wednesday with the Russian leader.

item.title: 🕵️ Australia’s innovation funds are multiplying, but the mission still isn’t clear | ASPI Strategist
item.description: When submissions to the government’s request for expressions of interest (REOI) for the Advanced Capability Investment (ACI) fund close this afternoon, officials will face a familiar challenge: not a shortage of proposals, but uncertainty about ...

item.title: 📍 Space Force awards first contracts for satellite threat warning radar payloads | Breaking Defense
item.description: Space RCO is already looking to start a follow-on effort to build smaller threat warning sensors for a wider set of Space Force birds.

item.title: 🌏 China is America’s Military Equal Now And In Any Future Fight, Marine General Warns | The War Zone
item.description: Faced with the rapidly growing China threat, the U.S. has to start hardening its bases now, said Marine Lt. Gen. Stephen Sklenka. The post China is America’s Military Equal Now And In Any Future Fight, Marine General Warns appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🕵️ Does liberal democracy drive inequalities? | Atlantic Council
item.description: There has been much debate about the relationship between liberal democracy and social and economic inequality, but data shows that free societies remain more equal than their less free counterparts. The post Does liberal democracy drive inequalities? appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 📍 How information and communication technologies can empower women in Senegal | Atlantic Council
item.description: Data and interview results show how information and communication technologies are empowering women in Senegal's informal economy. The post How information and communication technologies can empower women in Senegal appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 The clarity of the law differential: Channels to prosperity across income groups | Atlantic Council
item.description: Legal clarity increases prosperity through different mechanisms depending on a country's income level— high-income nations gain primarily through productivity and innovation, while lower-middle-income countries benefit most through credibility and reduced borrowing costs. The…

item.title: 🌍 Iran war has cost $25 billion so far, Pentagon official says | Defense News
item.description: Acting comptroller Jules Hurst III, testifying before the House Armed Services Committee, said that the bulk of the figure has been devoted to ordnance.

item.title: 🏛️ Sixty days in, Pentagon estimates $25B spent on Iran war | Defense One
item.description: The defense secretary and Joint Chiefs chairman appeared before Congress for the first time since strikes began Feb. 28.

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon leaders place $25 billion price tag on Operation Epic Fury | Breaking Defense
item.description: Members of the House Armed Services Committee also pressed Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on the recent firing of Army and Navy leaders.

item.title: 🏛️ Marines to field light loitering munition to operational units in ‘June timeframe’: Official | Breaking Defense
item.description: Currently OFP-L systems from three manufacturers are being tested by units at Dugway Proving Grounds outside of Salt Lake City.

item.title: 🌏 SPACECOM’s FY27 spending boost wish list? Empty. | Breaking Defense
item.description: US Space Command’s plan to ask for no additional FY27 funds follows a similar move by US Indo-Pacific Command.

item.title: 📍 Trump’s War Is Damaging U.S. Arms Exports to Allies | Foreign Policy
item.description: Deals are being canceled as stockpiles run short.

item.title: 📍 Can South Africa’s Apartheid-Era Negotiator Chart a Smooth Course in the U.S.? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Pretoria stakes high hopes on its controversial pick for ambassador to Washington.

item.title: 🏛️ Marines Realize They Can’t Depend On Army For Ballistic Missile Defense | The War Zone
item.description: The Marines currently rely on already strained Army units to provide defense against incoming ballistic missiles, which could be a big problem in the Pacific. The post Marines Realize They Can’t Depend On Army For Ballistic Missile Defense appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 The Global Economic Impact from the Iran Conflict | Foreign Policy
item.description: Gita Gopinath on how countries can make themselves more resilient.

item.title: 📍 Human Geography: The Strategic Edge in a Complex World | War on the Rocks
item.description: In 2024, Judd Devermont wrote, “Human Geography Is Mission-Critical,” where he argued that the United States should focus on behaviors and attitudes informed by human geography to craft better strategy. Two years later, we asked Judd to revisit his arguments. Image: Samuel…

item.title: 📍 What swap, Gulf? | Atlantic Council
item.description: Amidst the chaos of the conflict in the Middle East, there’s been a buzz around offering dollar currency swaps to allies. Those have traditionally been the territory of central banks, but this time around, Treasury is taking the lead. The post What swap, Gulf? appeared first on…
